overview: Hyvmind is a collaborative legal knowledge platform where users publish structured source graphs (ZIP files of markdown) to create and extend legal knowledge graphs. It features 2D/3D graph visualization, a public graphs browser, group chat per curation/swarm, and now an MCP-compatible public HTTP API so external language models can fetch published graph data via API keys.
features: Source graph publishing from structured ZIP files
5-level knowledge hierarchy: curation → swarm → location → law entity → interpretation entity
Public graphs browser grouped by curation and creator
Group chat channels per curation and swarm sub-channels
MCP-compatible HTTP API with tools manifest for LLM auto-discovery
Global API key management with rate limiting (100 req/min)
Read-only node details panel in public graph viewer
Collapsible filter panel with search and node type filtering
Force graph layout caching for stable diagrams across tab switches
